learn.core_idea: Blocking I/O hands off progress to device completion interrupts that wake sleeping processes.
mechanism_steps: IRQ completion wakes process and returns control.
learn.worked_example: Trace shows trap.enter -> io.submit -> proc.wakeup -> trap.return before further compute.
common_mistakes: Expecting blocked process to keep computing.
Confusing wakeup with immediate user-mode execution.
pre_challenge_checklist: I can identify io.submit and proc.wakeup.
I can explain why wakeup is required for progress.
I can verify trap return after completion.
